_meta.unit: S206 Cross-era seams (Blackfyre + Dark Sister lineages, dragon skulls)
_meta.note: Wire the F&B/Targaryen-history layer to the main-series graph via the two persistent Valyrian steel swords (both near-islanded) + the Red Keep dragon skulls. Every edge grounded in a VERBATIM book/fab/D&E chapter quote (book-citation overlay). Marquee cross-era traversals: dark-sister -> brynden-rivers (Bloodraven, already Bran's mentor beyond the Wall, tss); blackfyre -> daemon-i-blackfyre (the seed of the Blackfyre Rebellions, tmk) + barristan-selmy KILLS the last Blackfyre pretender maelys (adwd). Orchestrator-assembled from node prose + targeted greps; fresh-verified S206.

edges.quote: Visenya twice wielded Dark Sister in Aegon's defense when he was set upon by Dornish cutthroats
edges.note: Visenya bore Dark Sister, trained with it since childhood; wielded it defending Aegon during the First Dornish War.

edges.quote: Since Queen Visenya had previously gifted her son with Dark Sister, Prince Maegor now possessed both of the ancestral Valyrian steel swords of House Targaryen
edges.note: Visenya bestowed Dark Sister on her son Maegor (25 AC), giver = visenya-targaryen.

edges.quote: the ancestral Valyrian steel swords of House Targaryen
edges.note: Dark Sister is one of the two ancestral Valyrian steel swords of House Targaryen (with Blackfyre). Parallels blackfyre ANCESTRAL_WEAPON_OF house-blackfyre.

edges.quote: Jaehaerys unsheathed Dark Sister and vowed to end the reign of his usurping uncle
edges.note: The young Jaehaerys bore Dark Sister when proclaiming his claim at Storm's End (48 AC), against Maegor.

edges.quote: Baelon the Brave played his part in the slaughter, with Dark Sister in his hand
edges.note: Prince Baelon the Brave wielded Dark Sister (92 AC, avenging his brother Aemon on Tarth).

edges.quote: had been given Dark Sister by the Old King himself in recognition of his prowess
edges.note: Jaehaerys I (the Old King) gave Dark Sister to the Rogue Prince Daemon; giver = jaehaerys-i-targaryen.

edges.quote: Armed with Dark Sister, the prince made short work of his rival
edges.note: Daemon wielded Dark Sister through the War for the Stepstones and beyond.

edges.quote: In his hand was Dark Sister, the sword of Queen Visenya
edges.note: Daemon used Dark Sister to slay Aemond in the Battle Above the Gods Eye (the Dance).

edges.quote: Daemon ripped off his nephew's helm and drove the sword down into his blind eye, so hard the point came out the back of the young prince's throat
edges.note: Aemond Targaryen was slain with Dark Sister at the Battle Above the Gods Eye.

edges.quote: garbed in smoke and scarlet with Dark Sister on his hip
edges.note: MARQUEE cross-era seam: Bloodraven (Bran's mentor beyond the Wall) bore Dark Sister; Dunk saw it on the Street of Steel (205 AC). Threads Dark Sister F&B->D&E->main-series.

edges.quote: Armed with the Valyrian steel blade Blackfyre, he was counted amongst the greatest warriors of his age
edges.note: Aegon the Conqueror bore Blackfyre, the ancestral Targaryen Valyrian steel sword.

edges.quote: the king presented his father's sword, Blackfyre, to his brother
edges.note: King Aenys I granted Blackfyre to his more martial brother Maegor ('You are more fit to bear this blade'); giver = aenys-i-targaryen. A bequest that proved unwise.

edges.quote: taking off Gawen's old grey head himself with a single swing of Blackfyre
edges.note: Maegor the Cruel wielded Blackfyre throughout his reign; carried it into Pentoshi exile.

edges.quote: But Jaehaerys had Blackfyre in hand
edges.note: As king, Jaehaerys I bore Blackfyre; slew the rebel Borys Baratheon with it in single combat.

edges.quote: But Jaehaerys had Blackfyre in hand
edges.note: Jaehaerys used Blackfyre to cut down Borys Baratheon in single combat during the Vulture King war (Third Dornish War / Lord Rogar's War, 61 AC).

edges.quote: Why else would he put Blackfyre into his hand in place of Daeron's?
edges.note: MARQUEE (D&E seed): King Aegon IV the Unworthy bestowed the Conqueror's sword on his bastard son Daemon rather than his heir Daeron -- the seed from which the Blackfyre Rebellions grew; giver = aegon-iv-targaryen.

edges.quote: During the War of the Ninepenny Kings, Selmy had cut a bloody path through their ranks to slay the last of the Blackfyre Pretenders
edges.note: MARQUEE (main-series <- Blackfyre line): Ser Barristan Selmy slew Maelys the Monstrous (the last Blackfyre pretender; adwd-tyrion-02:145 names Maelys as the end of the male Blackfyre line), ending the Blackfyre threat.

edges.quote: During the War of the Ninepenny Kings, Selmy had cut a bloody path through their ranks to slay the last of the Blackfyre Pretenders
edges.note: Maelys the Monstrous (the last Blackfyre pretender per adwd-tyrion-02:145) died in the War of the Ninepenny Kings on the Stepstones.

edges.quote: he found the skulls in the dank cellar where they had been stored
edges.note: The dragon skulls (Balerion, Vhagar, Meraxes and 16 others) hung in the Red Keep throne room until Robert had them removed to the cellars -- the persistent physical trace of the F&B dragons in the main-series Red Keep.

edges.quote: so the bone seemed to shimmer in the light of his torch
edges.note: The dragon skulls are dragonbone -- black as onyx, unharmed by torch-fire.
